eliminate: Per-site static guard headcount as the default unit of sale
reduce: Quote-on-request opacity — publish indicative price bands
raise: Outcome-based-contract readiness for buyers who must tender that way
create: A white-label CCaaS channel for boutique agencies
A group cost-allocation model for internal BUs
A dormitory-specific 'Manless Security' package timed to DTS 2030

attack_plans.niche_name: One-contract command centre for dorm operators
attack_plans.why_gap: Certis and ISS can bundle but price for government scale and do not position to dormitories; Oneberry and Gabkotech own lean dorm security but are single-service. The integrated mid-market dorm slot is empty.
attack_plans.why_we_win: The command centre dispatches a security response and a pest/cleaning call-out from one queue. Existing Manless Security relationships give real FEDA fit; AB Associates supplies the trades — Elitez bids as one accountable partner.

gtm.channel: Direct sales to operations directors via DASL and existing accounts.
gtm.hook: An 'estate consolidation audit' as the door-opener.
gtm.pricing: Per-estate subscription from ~S$8k/month, portfolio discount above 3 estates.

attack_plans.niche_name: Zero-capex command centre for single-site dorms
attack_plans.why_gap: Oneberry's zero-upfront fee is the only clean mid-market entry — but it sells security alone, leaving the owner to source cleaning and pest separately. No competitor offers a single-site dorm one bundled, capex-free invoice.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Elitez matches the zero-capex monthly model and folds in FEDA reporting plus modular cleaning/pest from AB Associates — one invoice, one accountable partner. PSG/SPI pathways land the net price below a guard contract.

gtm.channel: Inbound plus targeted outreach to MOM FEDA-licensees; referrals from rank-1 accounts.
gtm.pricing: All-inclusive from ~S$15k/month, grant-support as the conversion hook.

attack_plans.niche_name: Command-centre IFM for mid-tier commercial MCSTs
attack_plans.why_gap: Certis and ISS have the integration but a price floor that excludes mid-tier MCST budgets; ICFM offers mid-market FM but has no command centre and no real security coordination. The mid-tier integrated slot is open.
attack_plans.why_we_win: The command centre coordinates security + cleaning + M&E + landscaping + pest under one SLA at mid-market pricing — the exact integration-breadth x price-access corner no competitor line reaches.

gtm.channel: Managing-agent partnerships and the strata-manager channel.
gtm.hook: A 'contract consolidation review' pitched at AGM re-tender season.
gtm.pricing: One command centre, one contract, one monthly report — without the enterprise price.

blue_ocean_summary: The category sells headcount and hides price. Elitez's uncontested space is the inversion: a transparently-priced, low-headcount, multi-site command layer sold as a guard-post replacement — with a dormitory package timed to a regulatory deadline no competitor is packaging against.
